concepts.category: One of: 'Most Important Fact', 'NCERT Line', 'Board Favourite', 'Memory Trick', 'Common Mistake', 'Units to remember'
concepts.title: Organisation Structure
concepts.description: An organisation structure defines the relationships between different departments and levels within an organisation. It is a framework that outlines the reporting relationships, lines of authority, and job responsibilities. Organisation structures can be functional, divisional, matrix, or hybrid, depending on the organisation's needs and objectives.

concepts.title: Line and Staff Organisation
concepts.description: Line organisation refers to the hierarchical structure of an organisation where each employee reports directly to their supervisor. Staff organisation refers to the support functions that provide expertise and advice to the line organisation. Both line and staff organisations coexist in a typical organisation.

concepts.title: Functional Organisation Structure
concepts.description: Functional Organisation Structure can be remembered using the mnemonic 'FEDCBA': Finance, Engineering, Design, Construction, Business, Administration. Each letter in FEDCBA represents a functional department in an organisation.

concepts.title: Identifying Organisation Structure
concepts.description: Students often mistake a functional organisation structure for a divisional organisation structure. To avoid this, focus on the basis of departmentalisation, which is the key differentiator between these two structures. Functional organisation is based on the function or department, while divisional organisation is based on the product or region.

concepts.title: Types of Organisation Structures
concepts.description: There are four main types of organisation structures: functional, divisional, matrix, and hybrid. Functional organisation structure is based on the function or department, divisional organisation structure is based on the product or region, matrix organisation structure is a combination of functional and divisional structures, and hybrid organisation structure is a combination of different organisation structures.

concepts.title: Advantages of Divisional Organisation
concepts.description: Divisional organisation structure has several advantages, including increased flexibility, better decision-making, and improved customer satisfaction. It allows for greater autonomy at the divisional level, enabling quicker response to changing market conditions.

concepts.title: Organisational Change
concepts.description: Organisational change refers to the process of modifying an organisation's structure, culture, or processes to adapt to changing internal or external factors. It can be driven by factors such as technological advancements, market changes, or organisational growth.

concepts.title: Importance of Organisation Structure
concepts.description: An organisation structure is essential for the efficient functioning of an organisation. It provides a framework for decision-making, communication, and coordination among employees. A well-designed organisation structure helps to improve productivity, reduce conflicts, and enhance overall performance.
